channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: Orchid Pharma, Dhanuka Labs Await NCLT Merger Verdict Today
item.description: Orchid Pharma and Dhanuka Laboratories await a key ruling today, March 18, 2026. The National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT) will announce its decision on their merger scheme, a critical step toward creating a larger, consolidated pharmaceutical entity in India.

item.title: GNG Electronics Secures ₹80 Crore Credit Line: Growth Fuel or Debt Risk?
item.description: GNG Electronics Limited has secured an ₹80 crore supplemental working capital facility from DBS Bank India Ltd, boosting its credit line. This move aims to support operations and financial flexibility. While the company shows strong revenue and profit growth within India's booming electronics sector, its increasing debt, negative operating cash flow, and high valuations are key concerns. Positive analyst ratings and past stock performance offer some support against these risks.

item.title: Ashish Kacholia Exits Cosmic CRF, Nears BEW Engineering Exit
item.description: Investor Ashish Kacholia is selling his stake in Cosmic CRF and moving towards exiting BEW Engineering. These portfolio adjustments happened alongside other significant stock trades, like promoter sales in Filatex Fashions and an investment in Electrosteel Castings. Kacholia's exits come as India's manufacturing and infrastructure sectors show strong growth.

item.title: PhysicsWallah Faces ₹263 Cr Tax Demand Despite Strong Q4 Results
item.description: PhysicsWallah reported a robust 33% increase in December quarter profit to ₹102.27 crore and revenue growth to ₹1,082.41 crore, alongside a 7.87% stock surge. However, the edtech firm has received a substantial ₹263.34 crore income tax demand notice for AY 2023-24, challenging the classification of investor funds as taxable income. The company intends to contest this assessment, signaling a potential protracted dispute as it navigates a competitive and consolidating edtech market.

item.title: India's Energy Security at Risk as Hormuz Crisis Strands Ships
item.description: Twenty-two Indian vessels carrying vital energy cargo remain stranded in the Persian Gulf due to escalating Middle East conflict, severely impacting India's energy security. While crude oil imports offer some flexibility through diversified sourcing, the nation's critical dependence on the Strait of Hormuz for LPG and LNG creates systemic risks, threatening supply, increasing prices, and potentially destabilizing key industrial and agricultural sectors.

item.title: Quality Issues Plague India's Highways: Complaints Jump 60% in FY26
item.description: Complaints about the quality of India's National Highways have jumped more than 60% in the fiscal year FY26, reaching 11,340 by March 12. This is the highest level in four years, even as government spending on roads increased. Minister Nitin Gadkari told Parliament the government is using penalties and new technology to push contractors for better work.

item.title: India Sends Warships to Guard Energy Routes Near Hormuz
item.description: India is increasing its naval security in the Arabian Sea and Gulf of Oman by sending more warships. Called 'Operation Sankalp', the deployment aims to protect Indian ships carrying essential energy supplies like LPG and crude oil. This action comes amid rising geopolitical tensions and disruptions at the Strait of Hormuz, showing India's focus on securing its energy lifeline.

item.title: Jones Act Waiver's Limited Impact on Oil Prices Questioned Amid Middle East Crisis
item.description: A past Jones Act waiver, intended to ease fuel costs during geopolitical turmoil, offered little relief and drew criticism. Today's Middle East conflict is far more severe, raising questions about the effectiveness of such measures for large supply shocks and highlighting the ongoing debate over industry protection.

item.title: US Inflation Surge: Hot PPI Data Puts Fed on Alert Amid Oil Spike
item.description: US wholesale prices climbed faster than expected in February, with the Producer Price Index (PPI) jumping 0.7% from the previous month. Core PPI, stripping out food and energy, also showed sticky inflation at 0.5% MoM and 3.9% YoY. Oil prices soared due to geopolitical tensions, nearing $104 for Brent crude and $94 for WTI. Markets reacted with volatility, closing mixed ahead of the Federal Reserve's upcoming policy meeting, as analysts noted persistent cost pressures and conflicting economic signals for the Fed.

item.title: S&P 500 Futures Trade 24/7 Globally for Non-US Investors
item.description: S&P Dow Jones Indices has licensed its S&P 500 index for perpetual futures contracts on the Hyperliquid blockchain through Trade[XYZ]. This allows eligible non-U.S. investors to trade the index around the clock, offering leveraged exposure and the ability to react instantly to market news outside traditional trading hours. The move aims to expand access to digital markets and merge traditional finance with crypto.

item.title: JSW Realty Stakes Rs 2,000 Cr on Mumbai Luxury Project
item.description: JSW Realty is developing the Malabar Court property in Mumbai in partnership with the Patel family, with revenue expected to exceed Rs 2,000 crore. This deal marks JSW Realty's entry into the ultra-luxury segment in a prime Mumbai location, reflecting JSW Group's growth strategy in a thriving real estate market. The project will compete in a busy market and requires substantial investment.

item.title: Energy Prices Surge on Geopolitics, Hitting Bitcoin Miners and Hash Rate
item.description: Escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East have driven a surge in global energy prices, directly impacting Bitcoin mining operations. The network's hash rate has fallen approximately 8% over the past week to around 920 EH/s, signaling increased pressure on miners. This environment is squeezing profitability, potentially pushing less efficient operations towards capitulation, and is expected to trigger a significant downward adjustment in network difficulty.

item.title: Senate Nears Crypto Bill Deal, Tightening Stablecoin Reward Rules
item.description: U.S. Senator Cynthia Lummis reports significant progress on the Digital Asset Market Clarity Act. Key compromises will restrict stablecoin rewards from being marketed like banking products, aiming to ease industry concerns. Although major debates are resolved, the bill faces challenges due to the packed 2026 election year Senate schedule and existing legislative disputes.

item.title: [Company Name] Raises ₹11.76 Cr Via Shares, Warrants, Loan Conversion
item.description: [Company Name]'s Board of Directors has approved significant capital-raising steps. The company will issue 11,49,998 shares and 72,49,998 warrants, bringing in ₹11.76 crore. Additionally, a ₹6.18 crore loan has been converted into equity. These moves boost the company's capital to ₹10.78 crore and include appointments like Mr. Arvinder Singh Kohli as Independent Director and Mr. Surinder Kalra as Internal Auditor to strengthen governance.

item.title: Kraken IPO Halted as Crypto Market Cools, Signals Industry Shift
item.description: Cryptocurrency exchange Kraken has temporarily paused its IPO. The company cited weak market conditions, including falling asset prices and lower trading. This pause highlights a major shift in the crypto industry, where companies looking to go public now face greater pressure to show they are stable and can generate steady profits, not just rapid growth. Kraken is waiting for better market sentiment, but crypto firms must now prove they are financially mature to list publicly.

item.title: PepsiCo India Adds 8 Electric Trucks to Greener Kosi-Pataudi Route
item.description: PepsiCo India has launched an 'EV Green Corridor' on the Kosi-Pataudi route, deploying eight electric container trucks with Kalyani Powertrain Ltd. This initiative targets 4.8 lakh electric kilometers annually and is part of PepsiCo India's broader efforts to decarbonize its supply chain, which includes converting over 400 distributor vehicles to electric and using 80+ CNG vehicles in the NCR region.

item.title: Raconteur Global Resources Plans ₹17.94 Cr Share and Warrant Raise
item.description: Raconteur Global Resources Limited's Board greenlit a significant capital infusion of ₹17.94 crore through a mix of preferential share allotment and warrant issuance. The company also strengthened its board and audit functions with new appointments. This move aims to bolster the company's financial base, though it introduces potential dilution for existing shareholders.

item.title: Tata Power Investors Back Six Key Business Transactions
item.description: Tata Power shareholders have overwhelmingly backed six important business transactions. The approvals, which concluded voting on March 18, 2026, passed with a strong majority, boosting shareholder confidence and allowing continued operations with group companies and partners like GRIDCO.

item.title: Paragon Footwear Targets Global White-Labeling, Boosts India Retail
item.description: Bengaluru-based Paragon Footwear is pursuing a dual growth strategy: expanding its international white-labeling operations to capture global brand demand and deepening its domestic retail footprint. With FY25 revenue at ₹1,652.1 crore and a return to profitability with ₹35.4 crore net profit, the company aims to leverage manufacturing strengths globally while reinforcing its market position in India through enhanced distribution and new retail formats. This multifaceted approach seeks to capitalize on both export opportunities and strong domestic consumption trends.

item.title: India Funds Small Hydro: Rs 2,585 Crore for 1,500 MW Capacity
item.description: The Union Cabinet approved a Rs 2,585 crore, five-year initiative targeting 1,500 MW of small hydropower development, seeking to unlock India's substantial untapped potential. Small hydro offers environmental and cost advantages over larger projects. The scheme introduces differentiated financial assistance for regions like the North East and projects are projected to attract Rs 15,000 crore in private investment, fostering clean energy, rural development, and domestic manufacturing under 'Atmanirbhar Bharat'.

item.title: India's Food Security at Risk as WTO Talks Stall Over Subsidies
item.description: World Trade Organization (WTO) talks for the upcoming MC14 conference are stuck on finding a permanent solution for India's food security programs, known as Public Stockholding (PSH). Draft texts released March 16th lack clear plans for PSH and the Special Safeguard Mechanism (SSM), which India and other developing nations need. A US push for new ways to discuss farm trade also risks delaying these important issues and could leave India's food security vulnerable to legal disputes.

item.title: Raconteur Raises ₹11.76 Cr, Boosts Board with New Director
item.description: Raconteur Global Resources has raised ₹11.76 crore through equity shares and warrants, boosting its capital. The company also appointed a new independent director and internal auditor, strengthening its board and financial oversight.

item.title: February PPI Surges; Inflation Fears Rise as Geopolitical Tensions Delay Rate Cuts
item.description: February's Producer Price Index (PPI) jumped 0.7% from the previous month, with annual inflation reaching 3.4%, the highest since February 2025. Core PPI also rose to 3.9% annually. These figures emerged amid escalating geopolitical tensions and surging oil prices, presenting a major hurdle for the Federal Reserve's inflation goals and delaying expectations for interest rate cuts, affecting stock markets.

item.title: Vedanta Eyes Third Interim Dividend for FY26 on Strong ₹7,807 Cr Profit
item.description: Vedanta Limited's board will meet on March 23, 2026, to consider a third interim dividend for FY2025-26. The company recently reported a strong Q3 FY25-26 with ₹7,807 crore profit and record revenue of ₹45,899 crore, fueled by high production volumes in its core businesses. Investors anticipate potential cash returns, continuing Vedanta's history of rewarding shareholders.

item.title: RBI Boosts Liquidity to Counter Tax Drains, Forex Sales
item.description: The Reserve Bank of India plans a three-day variable rate repo auction of ₹75,000 crore to manage liquidity pressures. Significant tax outflows, estimated at ₹2 trillion from advance taxes and ₹1 trillion from GST, have drastically reduced banking system surplus liquidity to approximately ₹75,483.63 crore as of March 16, 2026. This follows a recent seven-day VRR auction on March 17, 2026, where banks absorbed only ₹48,014 crore of the ₹1.5 trillion offered, indicating cautious demand despite tightening conditions. The RBI is also actively sterilizing foreign exchange interventions, which have led to a sharp decline in forex reserves, to mitigate their impact on rupee liquidity.

item.title: Nifty Posts 3rd Straight Gain Amid Profit-Taking, Weak Rupee Pressure
item.description: India's Nifty 50 index extended its winning streak to three sessions on Wednesday, closing up 196 points at 23,777. However, the gains were tempered by significant intraday profit-taking, which erased over 130 points from the peak. Persistent pressure on the Indian Rupee, driven by oil imports and foreign fund outflows, despite RBI intervention, added to market caution. While broader markets and sectors like Media and IT showed strength, the Nifty's continued trade below key moving averages signals potential for further downside.

item.title: Petronet LNG Appoints GAIL Chief Deepak Gupta to Board
item.description: Petronet LNG has appointed Deepak Gupta, Chairman & Managing Director of GAIL (India) Limited, as a Nominee Director to its Board, effective March 18, 2026. He replaces Sandeep Kumar Gupta, who retired. The move highlights ongoing promoter ties between the energy giants.

item.title: Vedanta Board Meets March 23 to Consider Third Interim Dividend; Record Date March 28
item.description: Vedanta Limited's board will meet on March 23, 2026, to consider approving a Third Interim Dividend for the Financial Year 2025-26. The company set March 28, 2026, as the record date for shareholders eligible for the dividend, if declared. Additionally, its trading window for company insiders closes March 19-25, 2026.

item.title: SEBI Fines 111 Brokers ₹1 Lakh for Promising Algo Returns
item.description: India's financial regulator, SEBI, has concluded proceedings against 111 stock brokers who settled for ₹1 lakh each. They were accused of linking with algorithmic trading platforms that advertised guaranteed profits, violating market rules. The settlement resolved cases initially filed against 122 brokers, including major firms like Zerodha and Angel One. This move highlights SEBI's strong stance against misleading marketing in the fast-growing algo trading space and its commitment to investor safety.

item.title: South Korea CBDC Pilot Expands Amid Regulatory Stalemate
item.description: The Bank of Korea's Project Hangang enters Phase 2, adding two banks to test digital deposit tokens on a wholesale CBDC infrastructure. This aggressive expansion aims to slash transaction fees for businesses and explore new use cases like government subsidies. However, the initiative proceeds against a backdrop of stalled Digital Asset Basic Act (DABA) legislation, embroiled in a dispute between the BOK and financial regulators over control of stablecoin issuance, raising questions about future regulatory clarity and adoption.

item.title: U.S. Eases Venezuela Oil Sanctions; Production Limits Cap Impact
item.description: The U.S. Treasury has eased some sanctions on Venezuela's oil sector, allowing state-owned PDVSA to sell crude directly. This move aims to help global oil supplies amid the Iran war and price swings. However, Venezuela's ability to ramp up production is severely limited by years of underinvestment, old infrastructure, and significant debt.

item.title: U GRO Capital to Decide on New Debt Issuance March 21
item.description: U GRO Capital Limited announced its Investment and Borrowing Committee will meet on March 21, 2026. The main goal is to consider approving a new debt issuance, Non-Convertible Debentures (NCDs) via private placement, to boost its financial resources.

item.title: U GRO Capital Buys Datasigns Tech to Boost MSME Embedded Finance
item.description: U GRO Capital has finalized its acquisition of Datasigns Technologies Private Limited (DTPL) for ₹38.23 crore, making it a wholly-owned subsidiary. This move significantly boosts UGRO Capital's embedded finance capabilities and digital credit delivery for MSMEs, with DTPL's MyShubhLife platform central to its growth plans.

item.title: Thomas Cook India: Rs 2.8 Lakh Penalty Dropped, Rs 16.1 Lakh GST Demand Confirmed
item.description: Thomas Cook India reported on March 18, 2026, that Mumbai tax authorities dropped a Rs 2.80 lakh penalty. However, Chennai's Commercial Tax Officer confirmed a Goods and Services Tax (GST) demand of Rs 16.13 lakh, plus Rs 17.17 lakh in interest and a Rs 16.13 lakh penalty. The company stated the Chennai order has no material financial or operational impact and is assessing next steps, following a history of tax-related orders.

item.title: Mangalam Cement Keeps CARE A+ Rating, Stable Outlook Amid Debt Adjustments
item.description: CARE Ratings reaffirmed Mangalam Cement Ltd.'s 'A+' Stable rating for long-term and 'A1+' for short-term bank facilities. While ratings remain strong, the company adjusted borrowing amounts, reducing long-term facilities and enhancing others.

item.title: Onix Renewable Launches ₹19.9 Cr Offer for Sarda Proteins' 19.28% Stake
item.description: Onix Renewable Limited and its associates are launching an open offer to buy up to 19.28% of Sarda Proteins Limited for about ₹19.90 crore at ₹115 per share. Following warrant conversions, the acquirer's stake will grow to 80.72%, making them part of Sarda Proteins' promoter group. This marks a significant shift, potentially integrating Sarda Proteins with a major renewable energy firm.

item.title: Alkem Labs to Build New ₹533 Crore Plant in Ujjain, Boosting Pharma Capacity
item.description: Alkem Laboratories has approved an investment of up to ₹533 crore to build a new, large-scale formulations manufacturing facility on 30 acres in Ujjain, Madhya Pradesh. This strategic expansion is set to significantly boost the Indian drugmaker's production capacity and support its future growth.

item.title: Hot Inflation Data, Oil Spikes Rattle Markets Ahead of Fed
item.description: US equities dipped on Wednesday as a hotter-than-expected Producer Price Index (PPI) reading for February intensified inflation concerns. Escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, leading to significant oil price spikes and disruption of key energy routes, further rattled investor sentiment. Markets are now keenly awaiting the Federal Reserve's policy decision and Chair Jerome Powell's commentary for guidance on future monetary policy in the face of these compounding inflationary pressures.

item.title: India Outbound FDI Drops Sharply Amid Global Uncertainty
item.description: India's outward foreign direct investment (FDI) fell to $2.76 billion in February 2026, a significant drop from $4.30 billion a year earlier. This decline across equity, loans, and guarantees suggests Indian companies are becoming more cautious about overseas investments due to global economic and geopolitical challenges. The trend marks a shift from previous years of strong international expansion.
